code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    fluent, natural‑language description.  The implementation uses richer
    templates, handles direction‑reversed predicates (e.g. backupPilot where the
    object is the person and the subject is the mission), and builds a short
    paragraph by joining clauses with periods instead of a single comma‑list.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal strings.
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is the astronaut.
    astronaut_templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)} citizen",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Updated templates for astronaut‑centric predicates.
    astronaut_templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: (
            f"{s} was an American"
            if "United States" in clean(o)
            else f"{s} was a {clean(o)} citizen"
        ),
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} was awarded the {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {clean(o)}",
        "fossil": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is a fossil from {clean(s)}",
    }

    # Additional templates for non‑astronaut subjects or generic relations
    other_templates = {
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
        "bird": lambda s, o: f"The {clean(o).lower()} is the state bird of {clean(s)}",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"The {clean(o)} was awarded to {clean(s)}",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} represented {clean(s)}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is also known as {clean(o)}",
        "cosparId": lambda s, o: f"The COSPAR ID of {clean(s)} is {clean(o)}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is in the UTC{clean(o)} time zone",
        # New templates for common political/leadership predicates
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the leader of {clean(s)}",
        "senators": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is a senator from {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the representative of {clean(s)}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} commanded the {clean(s)} mission",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a crew member of the {clean(s)} mission",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the backup pilot for the {clean(s)} mission",
    }

    # Group mission‑related information to produce a single coherent clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    mission_info = defaultdict(lambda: {"commander": [], "crewMembers": [], "backupPilot": []})

    astronaut_clauses = []
    # Collect special mappings for merging later.
    nationality_map = {}   # person -> country
    leader_map = {}        # country -> leader

    for triple in triples:
        if triple.predicate == "nationality":
            nationality_map[triple.subject] = clean(triple.object)
            continue
        if triple.predicate == "leader":
            leader_map[triple.subject] = clean(triple.object)
            continue

        if triple.predicate in astronaut_templates:
            astronaut_clauses.append(astronaut_templates[triple.predicate](triple.subject, triple.object))
        elif triple.predicate in ("commander", "crewMembers", "backupPilot"):
            mission_info[triple.subject][triple.predicate].append(triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic handling.
            astronaut_clauses.append(f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}")

    # Merge nationality + leader information when possible.
    for person, country in nationality_map.items():
        if country in leader_map:
            leader = leader_map[country]
            astronaut_clauses.append(f"{person} is a {country} citizen whose leader is {leader}")
            # Remove used leader entry so it is not emitted again.
            del leader_map[country]
        else:
            astronaut_clauses.append(f"{person} is from {country}")

    # Emit any remaining leader statements that were not merged.
    for country, leader in leader_map.items():
        astronaut_clauses.append(f"{leader} is the leader of {country}")

    # Build mission sentences.
    mission_sentences = []
    for mission, parts in mission_info.items():
        parts_clauses = []
        if parts["commander"]:
            commander = parts["commander"][0]
            parts_clauses.append(f"{commander} commanded the {mission} mission")
        if parts["crewMembers"]:
            crew = ", ".join(parts["crewMembers"][:-1]) + (" and " + parts["crewMembers"][-1] if len(parts["crewMembers"]) > 1 else parts["crewMembers"][0])
            parts_clauses.append(f"crew member {crew} on the {mission} mission")
        if parts["backupPilot"]:
            backup = ", ".join(parts["backupPilot"][:-1]) + (" and " + parts["backupPilot"][-1] if len(parts["backupPilot"]) > 1 else parts["backupPilot"][0])
            parts_clauses.append(f"backup pilot {backup} on the {mission} mission")
        # Combine parts with commas and 'and' before the last element.
        if parts_clauses:
            if len(parts_clauses) == 1:
                sentence = parts_clauses[0]
            else:
                sentence = ", ".join(parts_clauses[:-1]) + f", and {parts_clauses[-1]}"
            mission_sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all clauses into one paragraph.
    all_clauses = astronaut_clauses + mission_sentences
    if not all_clauses:
        return ""
    sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses)
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
